{"step_by_step_thinking": "Dengue, Rubella, and Measles are known to cause maculopapular rashes. Dengue can present with a rash that is maculopapular in nature. Rubella typically presents with a maculopapular rash that starts on the face and spreads downward. Measles also presents with a maculopapular rash that starts on the face and spreads throughout the body. However, Zoster, or shingles, is caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus and typically presents with a unilateral, painful, blistering rash along the distribution of a nerve. It does not typically present with a maculopapular rash.", "answer_choice": "D"}
